articles.title: Dollar Rallies on a Hot US PPI Report
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) rallied to a 1.5-week high on Wednesday, finishing up by 0.22%. The dollar moved higher on Wednesday on the stronger-than-expected US Apr PPI report, which was hawkish for Fed policy. The dollar also has safe-haven support on concerns that the US-Iran ceasefire may break down...

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: BoC tone tilts mildly hawkish – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists note the Bank of Canada’s (BoC) April Summary of Deliberations balanced US trade risks with inflation concerns.

articles.title: Kazaks: ECB will continue to decide meeting by meeting, based on incoming data
articles.expand: The European Central Bank Governing Council member Martins Kazaks said in a presentation during an event on Thursday, the central bank will continue to decide meeting by meeting and on the basis of incoming data.

articles.expand: New yuan loans for the month of April came in at -¥10 billion. Yes, that's another negative reading and one to follow up the supposed blip from July last year. articles.title: Canadian Dollar holds range as Trump-Xi summit, firm Oil prices balance USD outlook
articles.expand: USD/CAD trades around 1.3705 on Thursday at the time of redaction, virtually unchanged on the day, as markets price in mixed effects following news from the US-China summit.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Sideways versus US Dollar as BoJ turns hawkish – BBH

articles.title: US Dollar Index: Supported but capped on topside – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategist Christopher Wong highlights that hot United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Producer Price Index (PPI) pushed Treasury yields higher, but US Dollar Index (DXY) gains lacked strong follow-through, suggesting much inflation risk is already priced.

articles.expand: The Japanese Yen (JPY) reflects weakness against the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Thursday, with the USD/JPY pair holding onto the two-week high near 158.00. The pair remains firm due to the continued outperformance of the US Dollar.

articles.title: Euro flatlines above 1.1700 with ECB Lagarde, Trump-Xi summit on focus
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) is hovering right above 1.1700 against the Dollar (USD) on Thursday, about 0.65% lower so far this week.

articles.title: US Dollar: Gains extend on hotter US inflation – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Michael Wan notes that a stronger-than-expected United States (US) April Producer Price Index (PPI) print has driven a hawkish repricing of Federal Reserve (Fed) expectations, lifting Treasury yields and supporting the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Hovers around 0.7250 near nine-day EMA support
articles.expand: AUD/USD inches lower after registering modest gains in the previous day, trading around 0.7250 during the Asian hours on Thursday. The technical analysis of the daily chart indicates that the pair is remaining within the ascending channel, suggesting an ongoing bullish bias.

articles.title: British Pound holds above 1.3500, as UK GDP, manufacturing data beat expectations
articles.expand: The Pound (GBP) remains practically flat against the US Dollar (USD) on Thursday, trading at 1.3520 at the moment of writing, and consolidating losses after pulling back from 1.3650 highs earlier in the week.

articles.expand: UK economic growth held up better than expected in the first quarter of 2026, with GDP expanding 0.6% qoq in line with forecasts as stronger services activity helped offset softer industrial momentum. articles.title: ECB’s Lane Lays Out Case for June Hike Amid Global Energy Shock
articles.expand: ECB Chief Economist Philip Lane signaled on Wednesday that the Iran-related oil shock may require ECB tightening to prevent higher energy prices from spreading into wages, inflation expectations, and broader consumer prices. Speaking in London, Lane laid out the intellectual case for a likely June rate hike while acknowledging the Eurozone economy ...

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.

articles.title: Weekly Forex Forecast – AUD/USD, S&P 500 Index, NASDAQ 100 Index, Brent Crude Oil, Gasoline
